Formal Complaint Regarding Legal Representation by Robert Phan

Background
I retained the legal services of Robert Phan of Garcia and Phan for representation in two legal matters, engaging his services based on a personal referral and his claims of 20+ years of legal experience.

Specific Concerns with Legal Representation
1. Court Appearances and Professional Negligence
During the course of representation, I received multiple court notices indicating warrants for my arrest due to alleged failure to appear. Upon investigation, these warrants were a direct result of Mr. Phan's repeated failure to appear in court, despite assurances that he would handle all court dates on my behalf.

2. Ineffective Case Management
Contrary to initial confidence, Mr. Phan did not pursue optimal case resolution strategies:

*He prioritized "keeping me out of jail" over potentially dismissing cases
*Suggested proceeding to trial, explicitly noting increased expenses
*Indicated need to bring in additional counsel, suggesting inadequate expertise for the cases

3. Incomplete Case Review and Discovery
Critical issues were identified regarding case preparation:

*Mr. Phan requested I recall specific case dates, despite having this information in police reports
*Failed to thoroughly review discovery documents
*Claimed to have lost discovery materials for one of my cases

4. Missed Legal Opportunities
An independent attorney confirmed that one case potentially qualified for dismissal due to expired statute of limitations - an avenue Mr. Phan failed to explore.

Communication Breakdown
After expressing dissatisfaction with the services rendered, I requested a refund. My attempts to communicate via phone, email, and text were systematically ignored.

Requested Action
I am formally requesting a comprehensive review of the legal services provided by Robert Phan, with particular attention to:

1. Professional conduct
2. Case management practices
3. Client communication standards

Conclusion
The representation provided fell significantly below the professional standards expected of legal counsel, causing substantial professional and personal disruption.

Poor communication. Poor counseling. Unprofessional.

Edit: (3/19/24) So I contacted you again, and I assume your receptionist forwarded me to you, but yet you were too cowardly to answer? Pathetic.

Getting rear-ended is not fun.  If you get rear-ended in a serious collision like me, the event totals your car, leaves you without transportation and causes some serious medical problems.  If, on top of all that, you've recently moved to LA from Texas and don't have a support base, the aftermath can be extremely frightening.  In retrospect, I was very lucky that I'd met Sherif Edmond El Dabe a few months prior.

Since I'm not like the typical person I encountered during my time in LA and don't instantly presume friendship, I'd describe Sherif at the time of my accident as a friendly acquaintance, so I was a little worried about calling him out of the blue for advice.  My worries were instantly put to rest though.  Before I'd even contracted his services, Sherif provided me with in depth legal advice about the situation in which I'd unfortunately found myself.  He informed me of some good places to rent a temporary vehicle and even provided me with a list of doctors for treatment who practiced both around my residence and work.  Again, I was a Texas transplant who didn't have a lawyer much less a doctor.  It was these kind of personal touches which made his service so reassuring.

But he wasn't just a reassuring lawyer.  He was effective.  He was also timely.  In my situation, I was at the head of a multiple car collision.  Sherif explained that time was of the essence in this matter since I would not be the only person in need of restitution.  He moved quickly, and the amount of damages collected was more than fair.  I recommend his services here to anyone who unfortunately finds themselves in similar circumstances.  He is intelligent, well-spoken, approachable and instantly puts you at ease in a time of crisis.  His legal acumen is only matched by his earnest, calm and ingratiating demeanor.  I've heard enough jokes about lawyers in my life to know that this is a rare combination to find.

The best part about being able to work with them is they took care of everything. I did not need to be involved in any day to day and received a summary each month of where they were with my case. Where many attorneys will have continual conversations with their  clients to be able to run up your bill, the Buffington law firm does not operate that way. Rye, Kristi and Roger are all top-notch.  To find a firm who not only operates honestly, with integrity and takes a personal interest in every client is hard to find. I would recommend this firm to anyone who requires their legal services. Buffington Law Firm provided exceptional service for my probate estate / business litigation needs. Their professional and knowledgeable team resolved complex legal matters efficiently, offering strategic guidance that ensured successful outcomes. I highly recommend them for top-tier probate and litigation services.

I had an excellent experience working with Rye Mhtar. She was incredibly helpful, professional, and knowledgeable. She took the time to carefully explain everything and made sure all my concerns were addressed. Her dedication and attention to detail made a stressful situation much easier to navigate. I truly appreciate her outstanding service and highly recommend her to anyone in need of assistance.

In just a little over a year, BLF has carried my trust litigation all the way from settlement negotiations through Breslin mediation and trial that resulted in a court ruling in my favor. This lightning-speed resolution is a direct result of the diligent, expedient hard work of Super Lawyer Rye Mhtar (royal loyal regal legal gem), and the conferring direction of Roger Buffington through every chapter of the lawsuit. Just as one legal event was closing, Rye was turning the page on to the next step she pursued immediately on my behalf to my great benefit.

Both Rye and Roger were powerful in Court, speaking up for my interests affirmatively and without hesitation. The firm's loyalty to client, straightforward communications, uninterrupted attention to my case, and experience implementing the best plan for the client is the foundation of their trustworthiness and professionalism.

Isn't this what one hopes to find when seeking legal counsel? I hoped and I found. I would go nowhere else...

Thank you, BLF!!

Resolving my mother’s estate was a daunting and emotional task, especially given its relatively small value, which made it difficult to find an attorney willing to take the case. From the start, Roger Buffington and Rye Mhtar showed unparalleled compassion and professionalism.

BLF worked tirelessly to help me settle my matter out of court, ensuring that the legal fees were reasonable and aligned with the estate’s value. Their practical yet compassionate approach turned a potentially stressful experience into one that felt manageable and, ultimately, worthwhile.

They made me feel heard and valued, and in doing so, they reminded me that my mom mattered—not just as part of a legal case but as a person. If you’re looking for a law firm that combines expertise with genuine care for their clients, I wholeheartedly recommend Buffington Law Firm.

Check you bills and ask for receipts. Roger is having staffing issues from what he complained about. What a mess. Had to hire another attorney to finish the job. Make sure you check your bill to the documents provided and if you ask what it’s billed for you get charged.  Awful experience.
reviews.ownerResponse: The Firm usually doesn’t respond to Google reviews, but this one unfortunately requires a reply. However, we can only say so much due to client confidentiality and attorney-client privilege. To clarify, the person who wrote this review wasn’t the Firm’s client, but was communicating with our firm on behalf of the real client.  In any case, it became clear very quickly that the Firm could no longer continue representing the client (for reasons we cannot disclose, but which are unrelated to the unfounded claims in this review). We refunded all fees paid in the engagement, forwarded the entire case file to a new attorney selected by the client in the matter, answered numerous questions from this new attorney, and in every way assisted in the transfer of the case.  Why this course of action, that was ultimately a necessary conclusion of the engagement, has led to such a harsh and inaccurate review is absolutely unclear to us.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We have learned over the years that every negative review an attorney gets, especially regarding our firm, is tied to unreasonable and incorrect expectations of what a workers’ compensation attorney does or should be doing.
What you should reasonably expect from your attorney:
1. To always know what stage of the workers’ compensation process your case is in without having to ask your attorney. Our Case Status App provides this!

2. To always know the next step for your case so you can anticipate what is coming without having to ask your attorney. Our Case Status App provides this!

3. To be able to send us documents and not have to follow up to ask if we received the document. Our Case Status App provides this!

4. That your attorney will use his best efforts, judgement and knowledge to refer you to the best possible doctor to help you with your medical needs, once your case is accepted. After 30 years of practice, we know the good and the bad doctors in the system; the ones to go to and the ones to avoid.

5. That your attorney will negotiate the highest and best possible settlement for you and advise you the basis for any settlement offers. After 30 years of practice, this is where we really shine for our clients.

At Jackson & Jackson, we meet and exceed all of the above expectations of our clients as can be seen by the wonderful reviews we have received over the years.
What you should NOT expect from your attorney:
1. We understand after a work injury there is great financial pressure. We cannot help with your financial issues.
2. We understand that after a work injury you may be in an unbearable amount of pain. We cannot alleviate your pain issues.
3. We understand that after a work injury you may be experiencing a great amount of frustration regarding when and how you will be able to return to work. We cannot help with return-to-work issues.
Financial issues, pain issues and return to work issues are not work comp benefits. Our job is to get you to a good doctor, once your case is accepted, and demand that work comp pay you wage loss when you can’t work, once your case is accepted.
Ms. Akhibge, as you will recall, your case was completely denied, yet we still agreed to continue to represent you. We advised you that you needed to obtain your own medical care during the period of denial. So of course, you had to obtain all your medical care on your own. This is an unfair complaint against your legal team and is untrue.
All key medical reports are rated by our attorneys as necessary, and it is not necessary to rate all reports. Not sure how this is a “complaint” other than you fishing for something to complain about.
A settlement offer was made in late December, and it took the insurance adjuster months to provide the settlement documents. After they provided them, you decided to fire us. This is a legitimate complaint but not against your legal team but the insurance company that took so long. Again, your complaint is misplaced and an attack on the very people who are trying to help and serve you.
Your file was made available to you timely, just not on your time demands.

I am pleased to write a favorable review for Zev Brooks our attorney who prepared a Family Trust for my wife and I. Our interaction with Zev took place over three meetings. Two were virtual and the final one was in person along with a notary for document signing. Our meetings were always on-time and efficient with enough time allotted to deal with all issues. At each step Zev was meticulous in explaining all the nuances of the Trust document. He was receptive to questions both during our meetings and through emails and texts at other times. Preparing a Trust or a Will is a rather sobering experience as one ultimately must come to grips with end-of- life issues. Under Zev’s professional stewardship the task, while not fun, was a rewarding and worthwhile experience. Zev’s charge for the amount of time and effort he put into our Trust was fair and appropriate. I know lawyers are often disparaged for real or perceived. reasons.
